name: Chunk Data
desc: Sends a chunk section (16x16x16 blocks) with compressed block data, light values, and biome info.

fields.name: groundUpContinuous
fields.desc: True if this is a fresh chunk (sends biome + skylight)

fields.name: bitmask
fields.desc: Which 16-block sections are present (bit 0 = bottom section)

fields.name: data
fields.desc: Zlib-compressed chunk data (block IDs + block light + sky light + biomes)

notes: Each section in the bitmask contains: 4096 block IDs (2 bytes each), 2048 block light (4 bits per block), 2048 sky light (4 bits per block). For groundUp=true: 256 biome bytes at end. Data is zlib-compressed. XRay modules intercept this to replace block IDs or prevent chunk updates.

implementation.overview: S21PacketChunkData delivers compressed chunk section data to the client. Each chunk is 16x16x256 blocks, stored as 16 vertical sections of 16x16x16. The bitmask indicates which sections are in the packet — sections that haven't changed are omitted (saving bandwidth). Data is zlib-compressed. XRay modules intercept S21 to cancel chunk updates (revealing ores by not updating the world), or modify the bitmask to exclude certain sections (creating a selective render). CaveFinder modules analyze S21 data to locate cave systems. GroundUp=true means it's a full chunk (includes biomes), false means partial update.

modules.name: XRay / CaveFinder (Chunk Interception)
modules.purpose: Cancel S21PacketChunkData to prevent the client from updating the world with new chunks. This leaves ores visible (since the world was loaded with XRay texture pack making other blocks transparent). CaveFinder uncovers cave systems by analyzing chunk data.
modules.how_it_works: In EventReceivePacket, if the packet is S21PacketChunkData, the module cancels it. The client never updates the chunks, so any blocks that were previously visible remain visible even if they changed server-side. For CaveFinder, the module reads the compressed data and scans for air pockets surrounded by stone — these are exposed on the 'cave finder' overlay as colored blocks.
modules.vanilla_hook: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleChunkData(S21PacketChunkData) — decompresses data, creates/updates Chunk in WorldClient. If cancelled, the chunk in the world is never updated.
modules.anti_cheat_notes: XRay (cancelling S21) is DETECTED by anti-cheats that check if the client ever acknowledged chunk updates. Servers can check if the client has stale chunk data by tracking which chunk versions the client has received. Modern servers also use anti-XRay which sends fake ores in S21 data — the real ores are sent separately. Cancelling S21 on these servers means you never see the real ores. CaveFinder (reading S21 data) is passive and undetectable.

implementation.general_hooks:
Client: NetHandlerPlayClient.handleChunkData(S21PacketChunkData) — processes compressed chunk data and creates Chunk objects. PacketReceiveEvent fires before handler. XRay cancels it.

Server: Sent by PlayerManager whenever a chunk comes within the player's view distance.
implementation.client_variations:
Gugustus: XRay cancels S21. PostDisabler also cancels S21 in some modes.
Sigma 4.11: No specific S21 module.
Rise 6.0: XRay cancels S21.
Tenacity 6.0: CaveFinder reads S21 data to detect cave systems.
Mint B1: AntiLag cancels S21 to reduce client load.
Jigsaw 0.26: No specific S21 module.
